title: Two-Step Problems
essential_question: How can you solve two-step word problems using the four operations?
strategies: Read the problem twice. Identify what happens first and second.
Write an equation for each step, or one equation with two operations.
Check: does your answer make sense?

pretest.question: Kim has 24 stickers. She gives 6 away, then splits the rest equally among 3 friends. How many does each friend get?

learn.title: What Is a Two-Step Problem?
learn.content:
A two-step problem requires two operations to solve. You must complete Step 1 first, then use that result for Step 2. The operations can be: +, −, ×, or ÷ in any combination.

Example: 'Jake has 5 bags of 6 apples. He eats 3. How many left?'
Step 1: 5 × 6 = 30 (find total)
Step 2: 30 − 3 = 27 (subtract eaten)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Bar Model for Two Steps
learn.content:
Draw a bar model to organize a two-step problem. Example: 'Amy has 24 beads. She buys 12 more, then shares them equally among 6 friends.'
Bar 1: 24 + 12 = 36 (total beads)
Bar 2: 36 ÷ 6 = 6 (each friend gets)
The bar model shows what you know and what you need to find.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Write One Equation for Two Steps
learn.content:
You can write both steps as one equation using parentheses:
'(5 × 6) − 3 = n'
Solve inside parentheses first: 5 × 6 = 30
Then: 30 − 3 = 27, so n = 27.

Parentheses tell you which operation to do FIRST.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Identify the Operations
learn.content:
To solve any two-step problem, ask:
1. What do I need to find?
2. What information do I have?
3. What happens FIRST? (Step 1 operation)
4. What happens SECOND? (Step 2 operation)

Common patterns:
× then − (find total, then remove)
÷ then + (split into groups, then add)
+ then ÷ (combine, then share)
× then + (multiply, then add extra)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Check Your Answer
learn.content:
After solving a two-step problem, always check:
1. Did I complete BOTH steps?
2. Does my answer make sense with the story?
3. Did I use the right operations?

Example check: 'Kim gets 6 stickers each. Does 6 make sense if she started with 24 and gave 6 away?' → 24−6=18, 18÷3=6 ✓

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 두 단계 루틴 — 단계 분리하기
learn.content: 두 단계 문제는 한 번에 풀지 말고 두 단계로 나눔. 예: '24 스티커 → 6 빼기 → 3명에게 나누기'   단계 1) 24 − 6 = 18.   단계 2) 18 ÷ 3 = 6. 답: 한 명당 6장. 단계 1) 문제를 읽고 첫 번째 동작 찾기. 단계 2) 첫 번째 결과를 적기 (중간 답). 단계 3) 두 번째 동작 적용 → 최종 답. 흔한 실수 (M01): 한 단계만 풀고 끝냄 — 문제를 끝까지 읽고 두 단계 모두 처리.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 단어로 연산 알아내기 — 4가지 신호
learn.content: 문제 단어로 어떤 연산을 쓸지 결정:   + 더하기: '모두', '총', '합쳐', '받았다', '더 추가'   − 빼기: '남은', '나갔다', '주었다', '잃었다', '먹었다'   × 곱하기: '__개씩 N묶음', '__의 N배', '한 개당 ___이 N개'   ÷ 나누기: '똑같이 나누어', '__개씩 묶음', '__명에게 같은 수' 팁: 두 단계 문제는 단어 신호 두 개를 모두 찾아야 함.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 어림셈 검산 — 답이 합리적인가?
learn.content: 답을 적은 뒤 어림셈으로 검증: 답이 너무 크거나 작지 않은가? 예: '24 − 6 ÷ 3' → 24 − 2 = 22 (어림 25). 답 22 ✓. 예: 28 ÷ 7 = 4, +2 → 6. 어림: 30 ÷ 7 ≈ 4, +2 = 6 ✓. 🔍 검증 절차: 단계마다 어림 → 최종 답이 어림 ± 작은 차이인지 확인. 흔한 실수 (M01): 두 단계를 거꾸로 적용 (예: 먼저 ÷ 후 −를 해야 하는데 먼저 −) — 단어 순서대로 단계 분리.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.10 'Two-Step Problems' pp.301-304
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Two-Step Word Problems with Four Operations
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
